name: Women's Law Caucus
summary: Support members in their professional and personal growth, while fostering relationships through mentorship and networking. We strive to provide opportunities for leadership, service, and academic success.

name: Meeting and Events Club
summary: MSU Meeting and Events Club (MEC) is Michigan State University’s student chapter of the Professional Convention Management Association (PCMA) with the opportunity to become a student member of Meeting Professionals International (MPI).

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
summary: Asian Pacific American Law Students Association (APALSA) addresses the needs and concerns of the Asian Pacific American law students at MSU College of Law through various programs and fosters a sense of community through cultural and educational events.

name: Student Bar Association
summary: The student government for Michigan State University College of Law.

name: International Law Review
summary: The Michigan State International Law Review is a student-led legal journal at Michigan State University College of Law that is dedicated to publishing high quality, thought provoking articles in the field of international law.

name: Elliot Spoon Business and Securities Law Institute
summary: The Business and Securities Law Institute provides a forum for law students interested in business and securities law. BSLI offers members the opportunity to attend relevant and informative events.

name: New Student Orientation
summary: Our orientation programs are designed to connect incoming students and their families with resources and opportunities, begin to assist students in their transition to the university, and educate and empower students and family members.

name: Tax Law Society
summary: The purpose of the Tax Law Society shall be to foster a community of students who are interested in pursuing a wide variety of tax law related careers and  to promote the value of tax services in the practice of law.

name: Native American Law Students Association
summary: One of NALSA's main purposes is to foster and promote the professional needs and goals of Native Law Students and their allies.

name: Spartan Business Golf Club
summary: The purpose of this organization is to explore the business of golf and how to effectively use golf in business.

name: NSSC TRIO SSS Program
summary: The Trio SSS Program is for first-generation college students, those students who parents have not completed undergraduate studies at a four-year university. It also serves those who meet certain income criteria and students with documented disabilities.

name: Detroit M.A.D.E. Scholars Program
summary: Detroit M.A.D.E. Scholars Program focuses on college transition, graduation and career development for students who have graduated from Detroit public and charter high schools who also reside in the city of Detroit.

name: First-Generation Students at MSU Law
summary: This organization is based out of MSU College of Law for law students who identify as first-generation college students, first-generation professionals, and/or coming from low-income or working-class backgrounds.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America
summary: The Association of Latino Professionals For America helps provide resources to Hispanic students to exceed professionally while also fostering an engaging environment at Michigan State University.

name: Burgess Institute for Entrepreneurship & Innovation
summary: The Burgess Institute for Entrepreneurship & Innovation empowers students to lead lives of impact through entrepreneurship. We equip Spartans with the mindset, experience, and community they need to create meaningful change. Learn more at eship.msu.edu.

name: The Writing Center @ MSU
summary: The Writing Center at Michigan State University provides one-on-one and group writing consultations, various writing-specific workshops, and writing groups for graduate students and faculty.

name: Law College Student Veterans of America
summary: Our mission is to provide academic, social, and, professional support and opportunities, to military affiliated students and their families.

name: Broad International Student Council
summary: An organization focusing to nurture international students in various fields such as professional, career and personal development. We want to address the issues that come up with international students and be a centralized organization for them in broad

name: Concert Orchestra Student Association
summary: This organization is involved in MSU Concert Orchestra operations. These operations include advertising and publicity, community engagement, social media, recruitment, fundraising events, repertoire suggestions, conflict resolution, and social events.

name: MSU Federal Credit Union
summary: MSUFCU was established 82 years ago to provide a safe place where members could borrow and save money. Over the years, we've built a strong foundation of relationships with our members and students.

name: Graduate Recruitment Initiative Team
summary: GRIT is a STEM graduate student organization. The mission of GRIT is to increase the recruitment, retention, and long-term development of graduate students from traditionally underrepresented and marginalized backgrounds.

name: MSU Greenline
summary: MSU Greenline is the largest student telemarketing program in the B1G Ten Conference. With 150+ student employees, the program raises over 1 million dollars for the university annually.

name: Kappa Delta Pi
summary: Kappa Delta Pi is an international honor society in education that was founded in the year of 1911. The Epsilon Kappa Chapter of Kappa Delta Pi was founded on May 23, 1942. Invitation to Kappa Delta Pi is based on high academic achievement at the college

name: Office for International Students & Scholars
summary: The Office for International Students and Scholars (OISS) provide support to Michigan State University's international students, scholars and families.

name: Kappa Delta
summary: "Ta Kala Diokomen" - Let us strive for that which is honorable, beautiful, and highest.

name: SPDC Doctoral Student Society
summary: SPDC Doctoral Student Society is a family that brings everyone together to conquer challenges in life and academia. We welcome all doctoral students in SPDC and also future doctoral students.

name: Facility for Rare Isotope Beams (FRIB)
summary: FRIB will be a world-leading discovery facility, operated by MSU as a user facility for the Office of Nuclear Physics in the U.S. Department of Energy Office of Science.

name: MSU Tech Store
summary: The MSU Tech Store specializes in technology consultations, so that Spartans can feel confident they're getting the best tech for their needs. We  also operate an in-person Help Desk!

name: MSU Museum
summary: As the state’s first Smithsonian Affiliate, the MSU Museum is an innovative and experimental collaboratory that exists to catalyze creativity.

summary: Whether you are interested in selecting the right major, exploring career fields and industries, looking for a part-time job or internship, preparing for an interview, or networking with alumni and employers, our team at Career Services Network is here t

name: QT-Grad
summary: Building a supportive and welcoming community for all LGBTQ+ graduate students at MSU. We work to provide opportunities for professional development and mentorship among members and with LGBTQ+ members of the wider MSU community.

name: Spartan Bookstore
summary:
The Official MSU Bookstore. Your campus store for Course Materials and Spartan Gear!
Located between Wells Hall and Erickson Hall on Shaw Lane in the center of campus.

name: Eli and Edythe Broad Art Museum
summary: The MSU Broad Art Museum connects people with art through experiences that inspire curiosity and inquiry. Admission is always free!

name: Air Force ROTC
summary: AFROTC is a training program that prepares college students to become officers in the United States Air Force or United States Space Force.

summary: The Office of Cultural and Academic Transitions (OCAT) constructs supportive cultural, social and educational communities that actively involve students in learning.

summary: University Blood Initiative (UBI), national-level, offers ways and means for local blood centers to supplement their recruitment efforts and enhance their internal processes to better engage with larger pools of potential donors.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: This chapter of the National Society of Physics Students was established April 22, 1968. We are an academic organization run by and for undergraduate physics and astronomy students.

name: Students Demand Action at Michigan State University
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be established for the expressed purpose of organizing students at Michigan State University to advocate for gun violence prevention.

name: Freshman Class Council
summary: Freshman Class Council, a department within ASMSU, serves as the voice of the freshman class and coordinates activities, programs, and projects for the freshman class.

summary: BEAMS is the primary graduate student organization for the Department of Epidemiology and Biostatistics. BEAMS seeks to promote educational and professional development opportunities for graduate students in the Department.

summary: Passionate about bioengineering? Join Spartan SynBio where we compete in the iGEM competition to tackle real-world challenges and build solutions. Whether you're into CRISPR, bioreactors, or biotech, there's a place for you. Let’s engineer life!

summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide a meeting place for law students who are interested in family law matters. This organization will host special events like networking and guest speakers, and more to discuss topics in family law.

summary: WRAP is the graduate student group of MSU's Rhetoric & Writing (R&W) program. The purpose of WRAP shall be to engage Rhetoric & Writing students in social and scholarly activities, and help promote interaction and collaboration in the field.

name: Pi Kappa Phi
summary: Pi Kappa Phi is a fraternal organization dedicated to creating lifelong friendships, exceptional leaders, and uncommon opportunities.

summary: The mission of the Michigan State University Business & Law Association is to bring together members of the legal and business communities on campus and connect them with information, resources, and individuals that encompass both fields.

name: Spartan Zubaan
summary: Zubaan is an organization focused on engaging South Asian diasporan students of MSU (and other interested parties) in connecting with their culture and learning the basics of Hindi-Urdu linguistics.

name: Youth Movement Against Alzheimer's
summary: Promotes the understanding of Alzheimer's among youth and young adults by providing opportunities to help those affected by the disease.

name: HRLR Graduate Student Association
summary: The HRLR Graduate Student Association is designed to engage students interested in human resources, by facilitating learning, social, and professional development opportunities for its members.

name: Grand Rapids Emergency Medicine Interest Group
summary: This organization is dedicated to supporting CHM students aspiring to careers in emergency medicine by offering mentorship, educational resources, and networking opportunities that will prepare them for successful residency training.

name: Ladies of Elevation
summary: Ladies of Elevation empowers young girls and college women through community service, bonding, and sisterhood. We are creating an adolescent to young adult pipeline for underprivileged girls while maintaining unity amongst educated Michigan State Women.

name: Black and Broad
summary: We aim to support and represent Black business minded students. Create a safe space where Black Diaspora students can express their concerns. We also aim to be a pipeline of resources for students to succeed at the Eli Broad College of Business.

name: Survivor Michigan State
summary: A semester-long game based on the TV Show Survivor. Students compete in challenges and strategize to vote each other off to become sole survivor.

name: Spartan Chapter of the National Society of Black Physicists
summary: The purpose of this chapter is to promote the professional development and well-being of Black people pursuing degrees in physics within the local scientific community and region at large.

name: International Scholars Advisory Board
summary: The International Scholars Advisory Board (ISAB) is an Honors College leadership group that strives to create an inclusive and welcoming environment for high achieving international students at Michigan State University.

name: Transfer Student Success Center (TSSC)
summary: The Transfer Student Success Center is designed to build community and eliminate barriers to MSU for our Spartan transfer student community.

name: Latino Leaders in Policy
summary: An organization designated for any Latinx identifying MSU students who are seeking potential careers in policy,  to provide support in the for of career exposure, networking opportunities, and mentorship, scholarship, and internship experience.

name: Spartasoft Studio
summary: The purpose of this organization is to provide students an opportunity to actively participate in a semester-long game development project in a setting similar to a professional indie game development studio.

name: Global Surgery Student Alliance
summary: Michigan State's Chapter of The Global Surgery Student Alliance aims to bring together like-minded, pre-health students interested in learning about a career in surgery and improving global healthcare.

name: Environmental Studies and Sustainability United
summary: To increase support and engagement for students enrolled in the Environmental Studies and Sustainability major or minor.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Organization
summary: The purpose of CGSO is to foster communication of chemistry graduate students within the chemistry department and the university as a whole, and promote intellectual and professional development of graduate students.

name: They're So Dramatic MSU
summary: This organization will allow female and nonbinary individuals to lead and succeed in a welcoming and open theatre space while also allowing for people of any gender identity to participate and be involved in club events and meetings.

name: Autonomous Vehicles Club
summary: MSU's Autonomous Vehicles Club seeks to give students the opportunity to work on and around connected and autonomous vehicles. Open to all, aimed towards engineering & data science. Join us this fall as we dive into small-scale autonomous racing!

name: W.E.B DuBois Society
summary: The W.E.B. DuBois Society, founded in 1998 by Black students within James Madison College, is a vanguard organization dedicated to fostering a supportive community for Black students in public policy.

summary: Supporting non-traditional students in the MSU College of Law

summary: The Honors Students of Color Coalition (HSOCC) supports students in their transition to Michigan State University and fosters their continued success in college.

name: COM PA Medicine Student Society
summary: Official student organization of the PA program at the Michigan State College of Osteopathic Medicine.

name: American Indian Science and Engineering Society
summary: A national nonprofit organization focused on substantially increasing the representation of Indigenous peoples of North America and the Pacific Islands in science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM) studies and careers.

name: Official Pokemon GO Club
summary: MSU Pokémon GO is a club created to bring people together regarding the mobile game created by Niantic, Pokémon GO.

name: Imagine Software Club at MSU
summary: Our computer science consulting club focuses on the intersection of business & technology, providing an internship-like experience & helping students with their own projects. Learn project management & user experience, using AI, UX, UI, & other concepts

summary: This organization serves as the recruiting body for Michigan State University's Honors College. With students acting as ambassadors for the HC, they with prospective Honors College students through on-campus, off-campus, and virtual events.

summary: oSTEM at MSU supports connections between undergraduate students, graduate students, and MSU faculty working and studying in STEM.

name: Transaction Services Organization
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to create a welcoming culture for students to learn more about transaction services opportunities & build a strong group together that will support the next generation of students

summary: Our mission is to help First Generation Latino students succeed in college. This will be accomplished by providing MSU students with the resources and networks they require in order to thrive academically and professionally.

summary: The Research Safety Team (RST) at Michigan State University is an organization dedicated to promoting safety culture as a core departmental value in all academic and research endeavors within the Department of Chemistry.

summary: Our mission is to get all graduate/professional students, including chronically ill, disabled, and neurodivergent students, access to health care, support and institutional backing needed to complete their graduate program(s) while building a community.

summary: Administrative, business, and financial documents for Inclusive Access and Belonging groups at MSU.

name: Student-Made
summary: Student-Made's mission is to partner with Michigan State University in providing an effective and entirely student-run platform for entrepreneurial students to reach supporters on their campus. SM hosts pop-ups and creates a community for creators.

summary: Men of Action is a mentorship organization catered to young men at Michigan State University. Whether it being one-on-one support, group activities, or specialized workshops, our mentors are here to make young men feel comfortable and thrive on campus!

name: Data Science Club
summary:
- Create a community for students interested in the field of data science, data analysis, and AI/ML. 
- Provide professional development, networking, and career exploration opportunities. 
- Promote the use of ethical data usage in the development of AI

name: Helping Hands
summary: Summary: Members of our RSO will create tutoring and mentorship plans with an elementary, middle, and high school in the Lansing school district. Once a week members will go to each school and do as follows to provide students with extra educational aid.

summary: We provide activities and resources for students to be prepared for a successful career in the field of quantitative finance.

name: Cell and Molecular Biology Program Graduate Student Organization
summary: The Cell and Molecular Biology graduate student organization is a student-led organization dedicated to connecting graduate students within the CMB program.

name: Black Girls in Social Science
summary: Fostering an environment and community for Black girls to succeed socially at Michigan State University.

summary: The goal of the organization is to encourage interdisciplinary research and promote an understanding of the integral role of the Supply Chain within other academic and professional fields.

summary: The WAC (Women in Advertising & Communications) Mentoring Program, a collaborative initiative of the Greater Advertising Community and MSU, provides a college-to-career mentoring program for undergraduate students studying at Michigan State University.

summary: Our club works to educate and inform students on current events in astronomy and physics by inviting guest speakers, going on field trips, and hosting career panels.

name: Bright Futures

summary: Spartan Street Medicine is dedicated to bridging the gap in healthcare for our homeless community members in Lansing, Michigan.

name: Legal Technology Association

summary: This organization is designed to give students of the Charles Drew Scholar Program space to socialize and connect with all class levels enrolled. Students are invited to participate in all events and activities.

name: Partners In Health Engage MSU
summary: Global health advocacy

summary: Our mission is to nurture and cultivate the next generation of future physician leaders within the field of OB/GYN, as they begin making an impact on health equity, research, and advancements throughout the MSU CHM Late Clinical Experience.

name: Weezers, MSU
summary: MSU Weezers is a social club that meets once a week to participate in activities related to the band Weezer.

name: Shutter Squad, The

name: Star Wars Club

name: Gamma Epsilon Tau

name: Generations Connect

summary: 3D printing thermosetting epoxy-resin hardener systems and novel thermoplastic-nanoparticle systems.

name: Limitless Club at MSU

name: SPARK

summary: Diversity in Games is a club dedicated to discussing the role of diversity in the games industry from both a professional and consumer perspective.

summary: NOBCChE intends to address the diverse needs and interests of chemistry and chemical engineering students; provide access to supports and job connections for students; and promote awareness of the various fields involved with STEM majors.

summary: QuantZ: A premier student organization at Michigan State University dedicated to advancing knowledge in quantitative finance and STEM disciplines through innovation, collaboration, competition, and professional development.

name: Spartans Autonomous Racing Club

name: TechConnect
summary: We aim to organize tech meetups and have industry leaders give talk on industry trends and share hacks for getting internships/full time roles.

name: NMDP Michigan State Chapter
summary: MSU's National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP) chapter connects students with opportunities to save lives through marrow and stem cell donations. We raise awareness, host donor drives, and support patients in need of life-saving transplants.

name: Student Papers in Academic Research Collaborative
summary: MSU's first interdisciplinary undergraduate research journal.

name: ENGin at MSU
summary: ENGin connects English-speaking volunteers with English learners abroad for weekly video chat conversations. In just 1 hour a week, volunteers make a huge impact - helping their students break the language barrier, and offering long-lasting friendships.

name: Ambassadors Program for Global Health
summary: The Ambassador Program for Global Health/One Health (APGH) at MSU promotes global health awareness through lectures, study abroad, and volunteer activities. Members engage in educational and collaborative initiatives and scholarly developments.

name: Linguistics Student Organization
summary: The purpose of the LSO is to promote interest in and communication about linguistics and related fields. It aims to foster cohesiveness in the greater linguistics community by providing opportunities for academic, social, and professional development and

name: Spartans for Solutions, formerly A Look Into The Lab
summary: Cultivates student leaders who design and lead community service initiatives that create real impact. Through collaboration, innovation, and a commitment to service, members drive positive change in East Lansing and beyond.

name: Graduate Student Association in Anthropology
summary: The Graduate Student Association (GSA) within the Department of Anthropology is a student run, nonprofit organization designed to serve the needs and interests of graduate students in Anthropology at Michigan State University (MSU).

name: Humanities Social Network
summary: Our organization aims to foster a sense of community among humanities students through workshops, discussions, and social/professional networking events.

name: Center for Veterans and Military-Affiliated Students
summary: The Center for Veterans and Military-Affiliated Studentsr is dedicated to supporting the learning, development, and success of servicemembers, veterans, and their families at Michigan State University.

name: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Enterprise
summary: To foster a community where APIDA individuals can uplift their respective experiences both socially and professionally.

name: Designathon, MSU
summary: The MSU Designathon is a weekend-long “design marathon” where students can collaborate, learn, and compete. Students can attend speaker events and workshops from industry professionals and compete in teams in a 24-hour design challenge!

name: Gamma Iota Mu Professional Fraternity
summary: Professional Fraternity for people passionate about game development and looking to enter the industry.

name: Heard for Health
summary: This club aims to address and raise awareness about health disparities, especially among students interested in healthcare fields. We hope to advocate for people experiencing these issues by educating our peers and providing support and volunteerism.

name: Healthcare Law Society
summary: The purpose of the Society shall be to promote interest, education, and discussion related to health care law, policy, and ethics. The Society aims to provide a platform for members to engage in dialogue, networking opportunities, and educational events.

name: Professional and Public Writing Club
summary: P2W Club create's a supportive and welcoming environment for P2W majors, writing minors, and any other student interested in writing. We aim to promote inclusivity and foster a space where all students can feel comfortable writing and learning.

name: Bladesmithing, MSU
summary: The official bladesmithing team for Michigan State University.

name: Prevention, Outreach and Education (POE)
summary: The POE Department empowers the community to prevent gender-based violence through education, outreach, and social change. Efforts include training for faculty and staff, innovative programs for students, specialized RVSM training, community engagement.

name: Residential Business Community Advisory Committee
summary: The purpose of this student organization shall be to organize students in the Residential Business Community. The organization shall be carefully designed for students to voice opinions and give feedback on their experience in the RBC.

name: Veterans Adventure League
summary: V.A.L. is a group of veterans, reserve, and active duty that get together to play tabletop games—embracing camaraderie and inclusion.

name: Pharmacology and Toxicology Club, MSU
summary: The Pharmacology and Toxicology Club at MSU is devoted to social and scientific meetings to advance the knowledge and appreciation of PharmTox, built around the tenets of community, learning, experimentation, and preparation for work and higher ed.

summary: The Air and Space Law Society (ASLS) at MSU fosters scholarship and professional development in aerospace law. Through academic engagement, networking, and research, ASLS equips members to face legal challenges in aviation, space policy, and exploration.

name: CHM Hometown Healers
summary: HTH empowers rural girls in Michigan to explore and pursue careers in medicine through mentorship.

name: I Ink Therefore I Am: Spartan Printing Association
summary: Spartan Printing Association is an organization dedicated to the craft, teaching, history and preservation of letterpress printing. Utilizing printing facilities across campus, Spartan Printing Association aims to bring together a dedicated coalition.

name: La Casa RSO
summary: La Casa LLC offers MSU students engaging Spanish-language events, fostering community, cultural exchange, and language growth. It unites diverse Spanish-speaking identities in a unique, student-led space that enriches learning beyond the classroom.

name: People's Law Collective: MSU NLG
summary: An organization focused on coalition building amongst progressive undergraduate and graduate students at Michigan State University.

name: NEDA Campus Warriors
summary: NEDA Campus Warriors is a student-led initiative that raises awareness about eating disorders and body image issues through campus events, peer education, and fundraising for the National Eating Disorders Association.

name: HOSA - Future Health Professionals, Michigan State University
summary: Michigan State University's chapter for HOSA-Future Health Professionals, an international organization that provides future future healthcare workers with opportunities for healthcare-related competition, networking, and experience.

name: Black Researchers and Advocates for Nature, Culture, and Horticulture
summary: Empowering Black Students pursuing plant related career paths and plant hobbiest at Michigan State University!

summary: This organization aims to create a supportive community for Latinx social work students, providing opportunities for cultural engagement, professional development, and social networking.  This is a student-run organization for Latinx social work students

name: Rho Epsilon Iota
summary: Promoting the professional advancement of all aspiring real estate professionals by promoting the professional advancement of all aspiring real estate professionals by encouraging scholarship through commercial,residential, industrial, and ETC.

name: Spartan Housing Alliance
summary: Spartan Housing Alliance (SHA) is a student-led organization at Michigan State University advocating for affordable, safe, and accessible housing for students and residents alike through policy reform, education, and community action.

name: Diverse Leaders in Construction Association
summary: The purpose of this organization will be to foster a welcoming environment and build a sense of community amongst all students within the School of Planning Design and Construction.

name: Campus Compass
summary: Our organization is designed to support new MSU students with their transition to their new life and adventures in college. We will focus our attention on making everyone feel welcomed and creating an opportunities for our members to feel like a part MSU

name: Studio Art Development Club
summary: Helping students seize opportunities and make the transition to full-time artist.

name: Research Is Political
summary: A space for students interested in the wide world of research to stay up to date on how current government administrations impact the research field. RIP focuses on educating future researchers and providing voices to the underfunded research topics.

name: CHM Book Club - East Lansing
summary: Our mission is to foster a vibrant and inclusive community of enthusiastic readers who are committed to sharing a love of books and having engaging conversations.

name: SAMPE Michigan State University Student Chapter
summary: The SAMPE MSU Student Chapter connects Spartans with industry, research, and professional opportunities in advanced materials and processes through events, competitions, and networking.

name: Family Med Interest Group - GR
summary: The Family Medicine Interest Group at MSU CHM is dedicated to fostering interest, knowledge, and leadership in the field of family medicine. We strive to provide students with meaningful opportunities for mentorship and exploration of family medicine.

name: Fulbright Student Organization at MSU
summary: The Fulbright Student Organization at MSU aims to unite Fulbright Scholars, build a supportive community, and enrich their campus experience through cultural exchange, outreach, and academic/social events while raising awareness of Fulbright programs.

name: Korean Reading Club
summary: The organization aims to give students an opportunity to learn Korean culture and language through engaging in Korean literature and media regardless of language abilities.

name: Asian Pre-Law Association
summary: The APLA is a club to have representation within the Pre-Law community, and have events on campus where students can see the path of becoming a lawyer. The APLA is to not only attract pre-Law majors, but to every major.

name: Helping, Empowering, Advocating, Reaching Together
summary: This is a student-led organization for Human Development and Family Studies majors and others in the social science fields who want to make a difference in our community. We focus on volunteering, building connections, and creating opportunities.

name: Bonsai Club of MSU
summary: The MSU Bonsai Club is dedicated to the appreciation, creation, and cultivation of bonsai — the living art of miniature trees.

name: School of Social Work Doctoral Student Organization
summary: The goal of this organization is to uplift the values of the social work profession and provide support to all SSW graduate students by enriching the professional development of SSW graduate students and fostering community and inclusion within the SSW.

name: Neurosurgery Interest Group
summary: To spread knowledge, fervor, and encouragement about the field of neurological surgery, making it more approachable and accessible to all students, with a focus on mentorship, research, and community engagement.

name: Student Chapter of the American Institute of Professional Geologists, MSU
summary: The Michigan State University Student Chapter of the American Institute of Professional Geologists promotes professional growth in the area of geology and geoscience and creates networking opportunities for students to build professional relationships.

name: Broad Community Access to Resources & Essentials Support
summary: Broad CARES promotes student wellbeing within the Broad College of Business community by increasing access to basic needs resources, reducing barriers to help-seeking, and fostering a culture of care. We amplify the mission of the Broad Care Center.

name: Mahjong Club MSU
summary: The MSU mahjong club's goal is to create a space for people to meet up and play Mahjong. We hope to make playing Mahjong as easy as possible. Our club has meetings every Tuesday in Wells Hall B125/129.

name: Performance Showcase
summary: Performance Showcase allows MSU Theatre students the opportunity to participate in pre-professional, experiential learning opportunities through a performance showcase during their final semester at MSU.

name: Minecraft Coding Club
summary: The goal of MC^2 is to teach members to customize features inside of Minecraft! This will be done by teaching Spigot, Java and Resource Packs, to allow members to let their creativity flow!

name: Spartans Providing Advantages, Resources & Knowledge
summary: SPARK is a peer-led program that builds belonging and inclusivity. Through support for MBP faculty and five student committees, we empower student success and strengthen the Broad and MSU community.

name: Women in Conservation
summary: This RSO is a platform for women interested in the field of conservation to build a supportive community and explore opportunities within the field.

name: Futura Engineers
summary: An organization made to create a valuable atmosphere for Hispanic Latinas in Engineering. To empower and bring confidence into their lives and careers. A place to be transformed to better versions of yourself.

name: Foster Friends
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide support to children and families in the foster care community by partnering with 501(c)3 non-profit organizations located in Michigan.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
summary: MSU’s Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Chapter serves the community and legal profession through integrity, diversity, and innovation. By uniting future legal minds, the fraternity fosters scholarship, professional growth, and a lifelong commitment to justice.

name: Broad Business Law Association
summary: The Broad Business Law Association serves to support and empower undergraduate students from the Eli Broad College of Business at Michigan State University who are interested in pursuing careers at the intersection of business and law.

name: We Are Korean Educators (WAKE)
summary: WAKE aims to build a supportive community for Korean graduate students or graduate students who identify themselves with Korean ethnic backgrounds by facilitating social networking, academic collaboration, and professional development.

name: Kopp Foundation for Diabetes Chapter
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be KFD Michigan State University Chapter is to empower youth leadership in the fight against Type 1 Diabetes (T1D), promote awareness, education, and advocacy around diabetes care, support innovation and fundraising

name: Philosophy Graduate Students Association
summary: This is a professional organization for graduate students in MSU's philosophy department.

name: U.S Foreign Policy Forum at Michigan State
summary: U.S FPF at MSU is a student driven discussion based club. Each meeting we will discuss a specific current global issue and how (or if) the United States should approach global problems. We will also discuss the means and results of these decisions.

name: Royale, MSU
summary: The club strives to foster interest in the mobile game Clash Royale, offer educational and social activities for students, and support and bring together the Clash Royale community at MSU by hosting events while fostering Spartan Spirit.

name: Urology Student Interest Group
summary: USIG aspires to foster interest and excitement toward Urology amongst College of Human Medicine students.

name: Association of Women's Anesthesiologists, CHM
summary: AWA is dedicated to supporting, mentoring, and empowering women pursuing careers in anesthesiology. Our mission is to foster a strong community of future anesthesiologists by creating meaningful mentorship connections + facilitating networking.

name: Electrochemical Society Student Chapter, Michigan State University
summary: We host meetings to discuss field research, engage in science outreach and communication tasks for electrochemistry and adjacent fields. We aim to increase our presence on campus and schedule guest speakers and provide industry connections.

name: Action4AED
summary: Action4AED is a student organization dedicated to increasing access to Automated External Defibrillators (AEDs). Our mission is to educate the community on cardiac arrest prevention, first aid, and emergency response procedures.

name: Spartan Autonomous Robotics Team
summary: SAR is dedicated to the design, construction, and deployment of autonomous robotic systems, the development of its members as engineers and leaders, and the advancement of robotics culture at Michigan State University and in the surrounding community.
